element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Default weapon with moderate attack speed and strength balance

element_introduction_order.element: Power gauge (automatic fill when not attacking)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core mechanic: players choose between faster weak swings or slow strong attacks
Accessible through pause menu; binding spell (lowest cost) likely introduced first
Lowest magic point cost, tactical crowd control
Highest magic point cost, emergency tool against heavy opposition
Weaker attack but lower spell costs; strategic alternative to axe
Strongest damage output but highest spell costs; risk-reward tradeoff
Resource pickup for spell casting capability

element_introduction_order.element: Wing pickup (increases power gauge refill speed)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tactical enhancement for frequent strong attacks
Recurring support character offering strategic choices during stages
Implicit through narrative: journey to Blackhorn's lair across varied terrain

typical_relief_triggers: Using binding spell to freeze enemies temporarily
typical_tension_triggers: Magic gauge depletion forcing weapon-only combat
Power gauge management (choosing weak-fast vs strong-slow attacks)
Narrative escalation (Cutie's sacrifice, Blackhorn reveal as evil Astyanax)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Resource-driven tension cycles with player agency in weapon/spell selection creating decision-based pacing rhythm

difficulty_curve.early_game: Foundational mechanics introduction (axe, power gauge, health management) in safe context
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon upgrade choices (spear/sword tradeoffs) and magic spell mastery with escalating enemy density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Narrative climax with Cutie's sacrifice establishing stakes; final confrontation with Blackhorn (evil Astyanax doppelgänger) requiring synthesis of all mechanics
notable_spikes: Blackhorn identity revelation (mechanical complexity: fighting player's dark reflection)

notable_level_patterns.description: Progression through distinct themed environments (implied from arcade: forest, cave, lake, lift, castle, final battle) toward Blackhorn's lair, supporting narrative escalation
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Entire game journey from Remlia to Blackhorn's castle

notable_level_patterns.description: Pickup-driven mechanic where players choose between axe (balanced), spear (spell efficiency), or sword (damage) based on stage demands and inventory
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Ongoing throughout stages via Cutie encounters and power-up pickups

notable_level_patterns.description: Three-tiered spell system (binding: low cost utility, fire blast: mid-range, thunderbolt: high cost panic button) encouraging tactical resource management

sources_used: wikipedia: The Astyanax article (NES section for weapon/magic systems, plot, and gameplay mechanics)
